nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: You came on the dark. Good.
Stand closer to the speaker. The big ones make a shadow you can talk in.

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: Light shows it where to look. Sound shows it what you mean.
Under enough noise, meaning smears. The concerts aren't parties, friend. They're the only rooms in the sky.

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: The Accord says the outer system is closed for budget reasons. The ice-folk went quiet for health reasons. The moon went dark for supply reasons.
A lot of reasons, for one system. We're the people who noticed they all started the same decade.
The thing on Watchful Eye is awake, it takes notes, and everybody official has decided the safest thing to be is boring. We disagree. Loudly. On a schedule.

responses.buttonText: What do you want from me?

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: Tonight? Nothing. You're new, and new people are loud without knowing it.
Go do your jobs. Fly your routes. When you're carrying something worth carrying, come find the north stage on the dark.

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: The staircase. So they finally let it off the ice.
There's one person inside the ORG who still asks questions. We're getting things to them — and soon, getting THEM out.
Next set, north stage: we need ninety seconds nobody's listening. You own a guitar, don't you?

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: Because the crowd will watch you, the sky will watch you, and everything watching you is not watching us.
Congratulations. It's the first time being observed will ever have done anybody any good.

nodes.speaker: Pell
lines: Ninety seconds. Play anything. Play it like you mean it — meaning smears best when there's a lot of it.
And friend. Leave the phone in your ship.
